speed.label: Speed
speed.title: Speed
speed.description: Your speed attribute determins how fast your ship will fly.

sensors.label: Sensors
sensors.title: Sensors
sensors.description:
Sensor strength determines how near your ship needs to be to a target before you can scan it and how quickly a scan will complete. 

Additional intel can be revealed on scans with higher sensor scores.

energy_regen_rate.label: Energy Regen.
energy_regen_rate.title: Energy Regeneration
energy_regen_rate.description: Energy recharges at 1 energy per 5 seconds. Increasing your energy regen will increase the amount regenerated per tick.

power.label: Power
power.title: Power
power.description:
The amount of damage you do is directly tied to how much power your ship has. 

Offensive abilities that do damage will deal damage equal to a percentage of your total power alternatively, shield-restoring abilities will restore based on the caster's power.

If you plan on a tank, dps, or support role, power will be a primary stat.

targeting.label: Targeting
targeting.title: Targeting
targeting.description:
Targeting determins how effective your ship is at hitting a target. An attack roll consists of (among other variables) a targeting roll against the target's evasion roll.

NOTE: Your pilot level is a contributing factor in your targeting total.

threat_generation.label: Threat:
threat_generation.title: Threat
threat_generation.description:
The threat stat modifies how much threat actions you take generate. 

Attacks generate threat, but other abilities, like shield recharge abilities, or buffs also genreate threat.

Threat modifiers can increase or decrease the amount of threat your ship generates.

hacking.label: Hacking
hacking.title: Hacking
hacking.description:
Your hacking total indicates how effective you are at bypassing a ship or location's computer sytems.

Successful hacking actions will not put your ship in combat, nor will it draw agro (unless you're within the target's sensor range).

Failed hacking attemps, however, will generate a large amount of threat.

Hacking actions typically take longer to charge due to their damaging nature.

NOTE: Your pilot level is added to your Hacking total.

armor.label: Armor
armor.title: Armor
armor.description:
Armor are your ship's hit points. When your armor reaches zero, your ship will be destroyed.

Unlike shields, armor hit points do not regenerate and there are very few abilities that can restore armor.

Damaged armor can be repaired at any Recker Depot (for a fee).

shields.label: Shields
shields.title: Shields
shields.description:
Shields are your ship’s first layer of defense—all damage you receive will hit shields before armor.

Shields can recharge much more easily than armor, making increasing your shields vital.

shield_regen.label: Shield Regen
shield_regen.title: Shield Regeneration
shield_regen.description: Shield Regeneration dictates how quickly shields recover over time. Shields regenerate ticks occur every 5 seconds while in combat and 3 seconds while out of combat.

armor_regen.label: Armor Regen
armor_regen.title: Armor Regeneration
armor_regen.description: Armor Regeneration controls how quickly armor is restored over time. This stat is nearly impossible to obtain.

evasion.label: Evasion
evasion.title: Evasion
evasion.description:
The higher your evasion stat, the more difficult it will be to hit you.

Evasion counters targeting.

damage_reflect.label: Dmg. Reflect
damage_reflect.title: Damage Reflection
damage_reflect.description:
Damage reflect redirects a percentage of you've taken back to the attacker.

The amount reflected back is calculated AFTER any damage reduction stats.

damage_reduction.label: Dmg. Reduction
damage_reduction.title: Damage Reduction
damage_reduction.description:
Damage Reduction reduces the amount of damage your ship will receive.

This occurs BEFORE any damage reflection.

heat_resistance.label: Heat Resistance
heat_resistance.title: Heat Resistance
heat_resistance.description:
Your Heat Resistance rating (HR) determins how much radiation protection your ship has.

There are several environments throughout the System that are dangerous to standard ships. Adding mods with HR stats will help with your survivability in those area.

For example, a HR rating of 2,000 is needed to safely reach Mercury.

overclock.label: Overclock
overclock.title: Overclock
overclock.description: This stat affects your ability cooldown. 100% overclock will lower your GCD to .75 seconds.

stealth.label: Stealth
stealth.title: Stealth
stealth.description:
You steal rating indicates how likely your ship is to be deteceted by sensors.

Higher stealth ratings can effectly cloak your ship. However, dealing direct damage will degrade your stealth field.
